subject: [PATCH v5 22/36] mm/mempolicy: add MPOL_F_PRIVATE and zonelist selection

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a new flag, MPOL_F_PRIVATE, to the Linux memory policy system. This flag indicates that a policy contains a nodemask with private nodes and makes these nodes reachable via standard memory policies. The patch also introduces zonelist selection into the mpol allocator interfaces using the alloc_flags carried by page_alloc. With this change, a VMA can successfully service faults from private node memory, and cpuset rebinding does not affect private nodes. The patch is currently a no-op as nothing sets MPOL_F_PRIVATE yet, but it lays the groundwork for future use.
